title: Today My Body Is Allowed to Be Exactly as It Is
subtitle: An affirmation for accepting the body without conditions or timelines
excerpt: A short affirmation for accepting the body today, not once it is different. For the habit of granting the body conditional approval, tied to weight, shape, energy, or what it managed to do.

practice.whenToUse: Use first thing in the morning, especially on days when the body feels like a problem to be fixed rather than a home to live in.

content.text: A short affirmation for accepting the body as it is today, not once it meets a condition. The pattern of conditional body acceptance (allowed when thinner, fitter, more energetic, less in pain) keeps the body in a constant state of probation. This affirmation drops the conditions.
Stand with both feet on the floor, hand on your chest or belly. Say it slowly three times:
Today my body is allowed to be exactly as it is. I am not working toward permission. I have it now.
The first sentence is the permission. The second breaks the delay habit: the waiting for a future version of the body that earns approval. Both sentences are present-tense and unconditional.
